title:
Lesson 1:
I Love It / I Like It
description: 'Me encanta' = I love it (stronger than me gusta). 'Me gusta' = I like it. 'Me gusta mucho' = I like it a lot. These use indirect object pronouns — the subject is the thing, not you.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you try the new restaurant?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, me encanta. La comida es increíble.
dialogue.english: Yes, I love it. The food is incredible.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you like the dessert too?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Me gustó mucho. Voy a volver esta semana.
dialogue.english: I liked it a lot. I'm going back this week.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: ¡Yo también quiero ir! Me gusta probar lugares nuevos.
dialogue.english: I want to go too! I like trying new places.

title:
Lesson 2:
It Bothers Me / My Mood
description: 'Me molesta' = It bothers me. 'Me molesta mucho' = It really bothers me. 'Estoy de buen humor' = I'm in a good mood. 'Estoy de mal humor' = I'm in a bad mood.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you okay? You seem bothered.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Estoy de mal humor. Me molesta mucho el ruido.
dialogue.english: I'm in a bad mood. The noise really bothers me.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I understand. Can I do anything?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No, tranquila. Mañana estaré de buen humor.
dialogue.english: No, it's okay. Tomorrow I'll be in a good mood.
